description_long: This is a classic bentwood chair, often referred to as a Thonet-style chair, featuring a gracefully curved wooden frame and a round seat. The backrest is formed by two continuous, looping wooden rods that arch upward and connect at the top, providing both support and an elegant silhouette. The seat is made of woven cane, giving it a textured, airy appearance and a touch of traditional craftsmanship. Four slender, slightly splayed legs support the chair, connected by a circular stretcher for added stability. The wood has a dark, polished finish, while the cane seat is a lighter, natural tan color. This chair is lightweight, stackable, and commonly found in cafes, bistros, and urban outdoor or indoor seating areas.
description: A classic bentwood chair with a curved wooden frame and a woven cane seat, featuring a dark finish and elegant, looping backrest.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded cane seat and the full arch of the double-looped wooden backrest.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the chair's curved backrest and the thickness of the wooden frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the double-looped backrest and the connection points of the curved wood.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the seat's woven texture and the elegant curve of the backrest.
